spec_match.rationale: The page probes for anchor-position-follows-transforms support, but the actual visual demo is missing from the DOM, despite CSS rules being declared for it.

interactive.rationale: The page is entirely static except for on-load feature probes; the visual tracking demo container and move buttons are missing from the HTML structure.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The blurb describes the anchor transform-tracking concept well, but the page does not fully implement the visual components to back it up.

edge_cases.rationale: Since the interactive demonstration of moving anchors through transforms is absent, failure states and coordinate drift cannot be analyzed by the visitor.

references.rationale: The concept page is missing the required chromestatus.com/feature/5153932394102784 reference link in its 'see also' section.

design_system.rationale: Uses proper design system fonts, layout spacing, and clear green/red status indicators for the probes.

openQuestions.title: Add the missing anchor-demo HTML markup and move button interaction
openQuestions.detail: The style block contains CSS classes like .anchor-demo, .anchor-el, and .anchor-popover, but their corresponding HTML is completely missing from the body. Add the HTML container displaying an anchor element and a popover. Implement a click/toggle interaction on a button (using the styled .move-btn class) to shift the anchor element via CSS transforms so visitors can physically see the popover re-anchor itself in real time in supported browsers.

openQuestions.title: Cite the ChromeStatus entry link on the concept page
openQuestions.detail: The 'see also' section lists internal links but does not cite the official chromestatus.com/feature/5153932394102784 reference link. Add the required external link to fulfill showcase conformance guidelines.
